CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-13-2019 Continued To Mar-25-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Settlement
Off Calendar Oct-10-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances;
Kyle Nordrehaug, Esq. of Blumenthal Nordrehaug & Bhowmik, T:858.551.1223 ext. 1002, for plaintiff/moving party, on Court Call;
Evan R. Moses, Esq. of Ogletree Deakins Nash Smoak & Stewart PC, T:213.239.9800, for defendant, on Court Call.

RULING: Hearing held. The tentative ruling was not contested. The parties discussed the final approval hearing. After hearing, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFFS MELYNDA SINGH, NARCISSO SANTOR, ANNA SIBAL, JAY SINSON, ROBERT TAJANLANGIT's Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Settlement:

Plaintiffs Melynda Singh, et al.'s motion for preliminary approval of class settlement is granted. The supplemental declaration of counsel satisfactorily addresses all of the issues raised by the court at the August 14, 2019 hearing.

In addition, the final approval hearing is set for February 11, 2020 at 9:30am in Department 302. Plaintiff's counsel is to prepare and submit a proposed order.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-06-2019 Continued To Nov-27-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Settlement
Off Calendar Aug-14-2019 Continued To Oct-10-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Kyle Nordrehaug, Esq. of Blumenthal Nordrehaug Bhowmik De Blouw LLP, T:858.551.1223 ext.1002 for the petitioner;
Aaron H. Cole, Esq. of Ogletree Deakins Nash Smoak & Stewar PC, T:213.239.9800, for defendant, on Court Call.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on PLAINTIFFS MELYNDA SINGH, NARCISSO SANTOR, ANNA SIBAL, JAY SINSON, ROBERT TAJANLANGIT's Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Settlement:

Plaintiffs Melynda Singh, et al.'s motion for preliminary approval of class settlement is denied without prejudice to a further evidentiary showing, as follows:

1. Plaintiffs shall provide evidence regarding the experience of KCC Class Action Services sufficient for the Court to conclude that it is qualified to serve as settlement administrator.
2. Plaintiffs shall provide additional information to support the proposed service awards to the Plaintiff class representatives ($15,000 to Plaintiff Melynda Singh, $10,000 to the other four named plaintiffs). The moving papers do not provide any detailed individualized showing as to whether each class representative was deposed, whether he or she provided assistance to class counsel in connection with any aspect of the litigation (and, if so, for how many hours), attended the two mediation(s), etc. (Cf. In re Cellphone Termination Fee Cases (2010) 186 Cal.App.4th 1380, 1393-1395 [trial court did not abuse its discretion in approving $10,000 incentive awards to four class representatives based on evidence that they had, over the course of the litigation, assisted with investigation, responded to discovery requests, reviewed documents and pleadings, and testified either in deposition or at trial].)
3. Unless plaintiff Melynda Singh's participation in the litigation differed materially from that of the other class representatives, her proposed service payment shall be reduced, and the balance shall be allocated to the release of her individual claims.
4. In light of the size of the class (17,000), plaintiffs should address (a) how many class members are current employees of defendants; and (b) whether the proposed class notice procedure should include a website or other online component other than posting the notice itself on counsel's website.
5. Although no further showing is required at this time, in its motion for an award of attorneys' fees and costs to be heard at the time of final approval, class counsel shall submit declarations of counsel and/or other evidence sufficient to show counsel's lodestar so that the Court may conduct a cross-check of the reasonableness of the requested percentage-based fee. (See Laffitte v. Robert Half International, Inc. (2016) 1 Cal.5th 480, 503-506.)

Counsel shall file supplemental briefs no later than Oct-03-2019. Further hearing on the Motion For Preliminary Approval is set for Oct-10-2019 at 9:30 am in Department 302.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
